reviews.review_text: I own this game on every piece of hardware I own including my microwave. This is my favorite game ever and I have been playing it since before I gained conscious. I have beaten this game more times than I have breathed. I have not completed insane mode once.

Still one of the best couch co-op beat 'em ups around. The combat is simple to learn but has enough depth to keep things engaging, especially when leveling different characters. Packed with humor, memorable bosses, and chaotic multiplayer moments that somehow never get old.

have played this game since spring 2008 on my xbox 360. Skipping school, making skate videos and taking breaks at my duplex in western upstate NY. Don't really remember all that much besides the demo  ending with one of the characters pulling that light cord, which shut the entire screen off...ugh awww so heartbreaking lol and also whatever it cost then, seeming like a ♥♥♥♥ ton of money hahaha. So glad to see so many people have agreed with my sentiment as a 15 year old kid - this game rocks and deserves every ounce of adoration and love it receives. Castle Crashers for life!!!
If you think about it castle crashers is a harem game and the best one is the orange dress.
very fun game, very hand on insane mode, but worth it in the end to feel acomplished. very hard game to 100% but very enjoyable even back in 2008 on the xbox 360 :)

I may have less than an hour of playtime on here, but I have like 50 hours on my best friend's childhood Xbox 360. I'd add another 50 to my account if I had the time.

This game is the quintessential side scrolling beat em' up. It's fun, goofy, and provides a nice amount of customization.

Also it has a crocodile horse. Love that little guy.
оч имбовая игра, вспоминаю всего-то 50 часов в игре,а уже весело
One of my all time favorite childhood games. I've played through the full game multiple times and will continue to. Such a fun and well made game.

I've played this game countless of times with my friend since 2019. Never gets old. Pretty fun combat with lots of combos you can do, I love the green knight, he is sick. The level design and artstyle of this game are absolutely peak and it's loads of fun with friends, singleplayer is pretty fun too though.
Guys with controllers should really play this. It's like a 2D video game, But to save 4 princesses.

the game is fun but the final boss fight was SO ASS (spoiler warning) for the first phase half the time the boss is spamming the same move with a shield that damages you and your ENTIRE team when you hit it. when the shield is red you can damage him but he move in directions you cant attack in directly. then he turns into a giant spider that spams a move that launches and stuns you AND has two little minions always at his side. then he turns into a annoying balloon. then he just pulls out a random ass meteor sword for a whole phase. then turns into a balloon again. the game itself is fun but that bossfight is not very good imo
A great game to play with friends on the couch. A lot of variations too for such a small game.

This game will always be nostalgic for myself, I always get brought back to my cousin's room playing the 360 version on his tiny box TV with my sister him me and my uncle. This game never fails to make me play it in one sitting for as long as possible. Castle Crashers NEEDS to be kept alive!
i would 100% reccomend castle crashers to ANYONE who likes beat em up and hack and slash games. 10/10 game and even without dlc its amazing to play with your friends.

This game was a childhood treasure and I am so happy to revisit it now with the new DLC. I played for so long when I was a child, probably a thousand hours, and the best memory I have is beating my brothers in amounts of gold, level, amount of characters, etc. Though my brothers don't want to play with me now, I can confidently say that this is my top 1 game and I believe that won't change for a very long time.
it is a great game with one of the best DLC's ive ever seen in a long while its fun and even multiplayer and unique
This game is SO fun the tecks are cool and all of the workshop castle crashers. I feel like theres every thing in the workshop.

Castle Crashers has been a staple in my life since childhood when I found it as art on Newgrounds. Then it came to Xbox and I got to play it with all my friends.
Sometimes, like the Minecraft two week fad every group of gaming friends gets swept into, my friends and I have a habit of picking back up those console controllers and playing through the story as our old characters again.
When I saw the trailer for Painter Boss' Paradise, my jaw dropped and a part of me I haven't seen since those childhood days came fluttering back up to the surface. I wasted no time buying it and replaying the entire game myself just to see the updated graphics. Then I started to explore the Workshop, where my faithful passionate content creators post some of the most wild and hilarious characters, as well as some very real and welcomed suggestions.
In short, this game is absolutely perfect. For what it is. A fun and simple game meant for everybody. I've taught my 32 year old coworker and close friend who was never allowed to enjoy video games how to play this and I revel in the smile I see on his face. I then taught my girlfriend how to play this game, and she's gotten really good! To top that off, all three of us can enjoy it together and have room for a fourth person! Normally it takes something really messed up and dirty for that level of enjoyment to be shared by 3 adults...
If you are debating whether or not this game is worth whatever price you stumble upon it, stop wondering. This game is a CLASSIC and if you don't experience it you are missing out on quality gaming.
BUY THIS GAME

had this game on an xbox three sixty, then imported to the xbox one and then i started playing the steam version. And i have ALWAYS enjoyed this game. The challenges of the levels in insane mode and the boss fights are all really cool. Definitely would recommend.
Very fun beat-em-up, but you absolutely need friends with you when playing this game. It is absolutely balanced for multiple players. Playing solo can be a slog.

It's just as good as I remember it being way back in 2016 when I was 8 years old playing with my dad on the Xbox. My only complaint (which isn't even towards the game tbh), is that while it IS playable on mac, if you're using the Apple Magic Mouse for iMac, combing will be a lot harder, given that there isn't a real divider between left-click and right-click. Besides that, great story, great art style (especially with the new remastered update), and definitely more fun with friends (but still worth a shot to play by yourself!)

Castle Crashers is a timeless beat em up where you play as a knight going to save a crystal and 4 princesses, you will go around the world through many environments and unique enemies with all the dlc it is fairly priced, I would personally only buy on sale as it is an older game, but overall Castle Crashers is the best 2.5d beat em up i've played
Its crazy after so many years the game got an update i used to play thus game on the xbox 360 i a really good game
just have fun like a normal person, i said that to my sis who does not play games and she played for the first world and now the second

bought it on PC for the amazing dlc.
I still come back from time to time to continue playing.
almost any computer can run this game.
10/10

Castle Crashers is a classic indie game experience. The soundtrack is excellent, the humour is great, and a short campaign is carried by solid replayability. It's so much fun with friends. I'd say get it if you have someone to play with.

This game has been in my rotation going on darn well near 20 years now. It has fun, stylish graphics and real smooth gameplay. If you are a fan of the "beat em up" genre, this is exactly what you're looking for! 10/10
fun and easy to get into especially with the new update/ dlc. it can however get kind repeditive at times but if you look past that the game is great
This is a classic. I cannot stop gushing about this game and the memories it has given me. Please support Behemoth and grab this game.
I love this games side scrolling arcade game feel and art style. obsessed with trying to unlock all the characters. If you really like beat em up games then I would highly recommend this
top tier game. simple, nostalgic, and fun, plus takes a while if you're a completionist, worth every penny.

Truthfully, I mostly played Charlie Murder, which was based off Castle Crashers, but much edgier and in a more punk rock setting. Regardless, Castle Crashers is an absolute classic to those who played on the PS3 and Xbox 360. An iconic beat 'em up that plenty absolutely adore for it's unique artstyle, and simple, yet engaging gameplay.

Much like it says in the beginning though, I'd recommend playing with a controller.

The fact that a game that hails from a time in gaming and the internet that is long gone and still receives support today tells you everything you need about the game at its team. I never got to experience the peak of the old internet and the "newgrounds era" but this game feels like a representation of everything I missed out on by being like 5. I would setup my steam deck during lunch at school and just play with my friends for the whole time (hence the low hour count, SD doesn't track offline very well in my experience) and it was truly magical. Is the gameplay kinda repetitive? absolutely, but its engaging and put together in such a way that it doesn't detract from the main appeal of the game, beating bad guys up with friends. if you have a group of friends that's bored out of their minds, just play castle crashers, there's no better cure
Apsa ♥♥♥♥♥♥ lootely, Good farts, goof fights, good bewbs, it's all around a pretty good game from ye olden days when I was once a young lad
Really fun game, I was recommended it by a friend who grew up playing it as a kid and even now as adults its very enjoyable.
Classic couch co op , and the dlc made the game look and feel ten times better 10/10

Very fun game whether playing solo or with friends with funny comedic elements and great art. It's fun to look for all the pets and the different characters add a lot of replayability through their magic ability. The level system is fun too --- it allows you to choose your own playstyle very easily.

I have 200+ hours on my Xbox and while it doesn't deserve THAT much time it is still a classic and just makes me happy. It's just a feel good enjoyable experience especially to just ♥♥♥♥ around on with friends while you chat about anything and everything.
Endless amounts of playing for $15 dollars is insane. The best part is that it suits the differences for me and my friends' playtimes because there are so many characters to chose from.
Fun. A classic. Lovely to play local co-op, especially with children.

Yes and me and my friend TiraMesa played it and it was good. We kept stealing food off of each other and I stomped out Necromancer! Troll Mother is easy on insane mode those people saying "Its too hard" are being dramatic

Absolute classic. I didn't grow up with it but I picked it up because it was cheap and I could convince friends to play it. Though the combat gets repetitive on multiple play throughs, I still enjoyed it for a couple of runs both solo and with friends.

my favorite game of all time, i love it so much. silly little game where you go around beating up waves of enemies as you progress through the level. lots of fun levels with cool fights and enjoyable jokes, even with the slightly dated humor at time. there are unfortunately some (not that many) elements of the game being rushed, which does suck but it doesn't affect how the game is or anything very much. it's mainly just an issue with magic, since a lot of the characters share the exact same magic since they didnt get around to adding their own. but the amazing art style, the fun game concept, the humor, and the replayability make this my favorite game of all time, and make me strongly wish for a sequel that shows the true potential this game has. 10/10
this game is gorgeous. nothing brings me more excitement and joy then hearing my friends say "hop on castle crashers" everything about this game is beautiful.
Amazing game with lots of content and challenges. 100% recommend.

My first encounter with Castle Crashers is somewhere around 2013 at my friends place. We played it day and night, alongside with Toy Story 3. When I installed Steam on my old PC one of the first purchases was Castle Crashers and I put 100+ hours on that account (and who knows how many on the friends account), but I've lost access to that account and Steam support was not able to help me recover it. I stopped gaming for a few years and got a laptop 2 months ago that can't run much games on it, so I thought what to play and I remembered Castle Crashers exists so I gave it a look once again and I see that they added new DLC to 2008 videogame, I was amazed and bought it instantly. I do not regret it one bit, 100% recommend that you buy this game, it's nothing too special compared to the games we have today, but it has it's charm and some replay value to it, you can finish it in one sitting around 5-6 hours solo or up to 4 of your friends. Buy it.
